_scoring_convention.political_risk_score: 0–100 where 100 = zero political risk (higher is better)
_scoring_convention.operational_drag_score: 0–100 where 100 = maximum drag / worst efficiency (higher is worse)
_scoring_convention.rag_convention: GREEN = proceed, AMBER = proceed with conditions, RED = material concern

jurisdiction.name: Poland
jurisdiction.currency_eur_rate_source: NOTE: PLN/EUR is volatile — verify against live FX before use. Indicative rate: PLN 1 = EUR 0.235 (approx. EUR 1 = PLN 4.25).
jurisdiction.data_staleness_warning: Polish OZE auction clearing prices (ceny aukcyjne) change with each auction round (held by URE). PLN/EUR FX rate is volatile. Wind turbine setback rules have recently reformed (March 2023) — verify current Ustawa wiatrakowa status. All figures require verification against current URE publications.

primary_energy_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 20 lutego 2015 r. o odnawialnych źródłach energii (OZE Act — Ustawa OZE)
primary_energy_legislation.relevance: Primary RES legislation; OZE auction framework (aukcje OZE); two-way CfD mechanism (kontrakty różnicowe); URE as regulator

primary_energy_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 10 kwietnia 1997 r. Prawo energetyczne (Energy Law)
primary_energy_legislation.relevance: Grid connection framework; licensing; distribution network obligations; URE regulatory powers

primary_energy_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 2 marca 2023 r. o zmianie ustawy o inwestycjach w zakresie elektrowni wiatrowych
primary_energy_legislation.relevance: Wind turbine setback reform — reduced setback from 10H to 700m minimum; critical for onshore wind market reopening

permitting_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 20 maja 2016 r. o inwestycjach w zakresie elektrowni wiatrowych (Ustawa wiatrakowa / 10H Law)
permitting_legislation.relevance: Wind turbine siting rules; now amended to 700m minimum (March 2023). Land use plan (MPZP) requirement for all wind projects.

permitting_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 3 października 2008 r. o udostępnianiu informacji o środowisku... (OOŚ Act)
permitting_legislation.relevance: Environmental impact assessment (Ocena Oddziaływania na Środowisko — OOŚ) procedure

permitting_legislation.instrument: Prawo budowlane (Construction Law, Act of 7 July 1994 as amended)
permitting_legislation.relevance: Building permit (pozwolenie na budowę) requirements; wind turbine categorisation

tax_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 15 lutego 1992 r. o podatku dochodowym od osób prawnych (CIT Act)
tax_legislation.relevance: Corporate income tax — 19% standard rate; 9% for small taxpayers

tax_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 12 stycznia 1991 r. o podatkach i opłatach lokalnych (Local Taxes Act)
tax_legislation.relevance: Podatek od nieruchomości (property tax) on wind turbines — scope and rate subject to legislative change history (2017 reversal)

subsidy_control_legislation.relevance: EC framework for OZE auction scheme compatibility

fdi_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 24 lipca 2015 r. o kontroli niektórych inwestycji (Investment Control Act)
fdi_legislation.relevance: Screening for acquisitions in strategic sectors; energy included; amended multiple times
EU-level cooperation; Poland notifies EC for qualifying transactions

permitting._notes: Poland's wind turbine permitting was effectively halted between 2016-2023 by the Ustawa wiatrakowa (10H law). The March 2023 reform reducing the setback to 700m has reopened the market — but implementation requires updated MPZP (local spatial plans), which take 1–3 years to adopt. Full market reopening is a 2024–2027 timeline event.

solar_utility.primary_authority: Starostwo powiatowe (county office) for building permit; Regionalna Dyrekcja Ochrony Środowiska (RDOŚ) for EIA
solar_utility.primary_process: OOŚ (environmental impact assessment) if above threshold; pozwolenie na budowę (building permit); grid connection application to OSD (distribution network operator) or PSE (transmission)
solar_utility.legal_basis: OOŚ Act; Prawo budowlane; Ustawa OZE
solar_utility.mpzp_requirement: Ground-mounted solar requires MPZP (Miejscowy Plan Zagospodarowania Przestrzennego — local spatial plan) designation as admissible land use, or individual planning decision (decyzja WZ). MPZP adoption takes 1–3 years in many municipalities.
solar_utility.appeal_mechanism: Samorządowe Kolegium Odwoławcze (SKO — Administrative Appeal Board) → Wojewódzki Sąd Administracyjny (WSA) → Naczelny Sąd Administracyjny (NSA)
solar_utility.notes: Poland has large tracts of agricultural and post-industrial land suitable for solar. Permitting is manageable. Grid connection is the primary constraint (see grid_connection section). Strong solar growth trajectory post-2020.

onshore_wind.primary_authority: RDOŚ (for OOŚ); Starostwo powiatowe (for pozwolenie na budowę); Gmina (for MPZP adoption)
onshore_wind.primary_process: MPZP adoption (mandatory under Ustawa wiatrakowa — cannot use individual decyzja WZ for wind turbines); OOŚ; pozwolenie na budowę
onshore_wind.legal_basis: Ustawa wiatrakowa (Ustawa z dnia 20 maja 2016 r. as amended by Ustawa z dnia 2 marca 2023 r.); Prawo budowlane; OOŚ Act

setback_rule.previous_rule: 10H (10× hub height) — effectively 1,500–2,000m for modern turbines
setback_rule.legal_basis: Ustawa z dnia 2 marca 2023 r. o zmianie ustawy o inwestycjach w zakresie elektrowni wiatrowych (Dz.U. 2023 poz. 553)
setback_rule.implementation_challenge: Reform requires each gmina to adopt a new MPZP designating wind-eligible areas at 700m setback. As of 2026, many gminy are still in the MPZP adoption process — the permitting gateway only opens as MPZP are adopted.
setback_rule.rural_areas_note: Approximately 12–15% of Poland's land area is within 700m setback of residential buildings — significant capacity unlocked vs 10H but MPZP timing is the binding constraint

onshore_wind.notes: Poland's onshore wind market is reopening after 7 years of effective dormancy under 10H. The 700m reform is substantial. Primary delays now are MPZP adoption timelines (gmina by gmina) and grid connection queue — not the permitting procedure itself once MPZP is in place.

offshore_wind.primary_authority: Minister Klimatu i Środowiska (Minister of Climate and Environment) for offshore permits; URE for grid; Urząd Morski (Maritime Office) for sea area
offshore_wind.primary_process: Pozwolenie lokalizacyjne (location permit) + pozwolenie na budowę sztucznej wyspy/instalacji (construction permit for offshore structure); grid connection coordination
offshore_wind.legal_basis: Ustawa z dnia 17 grudnia 2020 r. o promowaniu wytwarzania energii elektrycznej w morskich farmach wiatrowych (Offshore Wind Act — ustawa offshore)
offshore_wind.phase_1_projects: Phase 1 (2022-2027): 5.9GW pre-qualified offshore projects with guaranteed contract for difference prices allocated by URE. Major developers: Orlen Neptun, Equinor/Polenergia (Bałtyk I/II/III), PKN Orlen/Northland (Baltic Power). Phase 1 uses administratively set contract prices, not competitive auction.
offshore_wind.phase_2_onwards: Phase 2 (from 2025 onwards): competitive OZE-M offshore auctions. Prices set competitively.
offshore_wind.notes: Poland's offshore wind market is one of Europe's most exciting emerging markets. Phase 1 projects have guaranteed support but complex supply chain and port infrastructure challenges. Baltic Sea is shallower than North Sea — fixed-bottom technology viable for most Polish zone. Polish industrial port capacity (Gdańsk, Gdynia) is being expanded.

grid_connection.transmission_operator: PSE S.A. (Polskie Sieci Elektroenergetyczne — Polish Power Grid)
grid_connection.distribution_operators: Enea Operator, Energa Operator, PGE Dystrybucja, Tauron Dystrybucja, innogy Polska
grid_connection.regulatory_authority: URE — Urząd Regulacji Energetyki (Energy Regulatory Office)
grid_connection.legal_basis: Prawo energetyczne (Energy Law); Instrukcja Ruchu i Eksploatacji Sieci Przesyłowej (IRiESP — Transmission Grid Code) published by PSE

connection_queue.description: Poland faces a severe grid connection queue for new renewable energy projects — estimates suggest thousands of MW of solar and wind projects are awaiting connection capacity at distribution level.
connection_queue.cause: Rapid solar growth (from near-zero to >15GW installed 2019-2024) has saturated distribution grid in many areas. Transmission grid investment lagging.
connection_queue.wait_time_years_estimate: 2–7 years depending on voltage level and area

grid_connection.notes: Poland's coal-heavy grid requires significant transformation. High-voltage DC interconnectors (with Germany, Lithuania, Sweden via Baltic cable) are important but PSE capacity planning has not kept pace with renewable growth. Coal plant dispatch priority creates renewable curtailment in some periods.

support_mechanisms._rule_3a_note: Rule 3A: Poland's OZE auction mechanism (aukcje OZE) is a TWO-WAY contract for difference (kontrakt różnicowy). When the reference market price (TGE day-ahead) falls below the auction clearing price (cena aukcyjna), the generator receives the difference. When the reference market price EXCEEDS the clearing price, the generator PAYS BACK the excess to URE. This is equivalent to the UK CfD and Italy's FER1 — both upside capped and downside protected.
support_mechanisms.primary_mechanism: OZE Aukcje (Renewable Energy Source Auctions) — Two-Way CfD
support_mechanisms.administrator: URE — Urząd Regulacji Energetyki (Energy Regulatory Office)
support_mechanisms.legal_basis: Ustawa OZE Arts. 72–98 (auction procedure); Arts. 93–102 (contract for difference settlement); Rozporządzenie Ministra Klimatu on auction parameters

mechanism_description.summary: Generator wins OZE auction at clearing price (cena aukcyjna) denominated in PLN/MWh. Generator sells electricity on TGE (Towarowa Giełda Energii — Polish Power Exchange) spot market. When TGE price < cena aukcyjna: generator receives difference from OZE settlement system. When TGE price > cena aukcyjna: generator PAYS BACK excess. Two-way CfD equivalent to UK model.
mechanism_description.upside_treatment: CAPPED via clawback — excess above cena aukcyjna paid back to OZE system (funded by consumer obligations on electricity suppliers).
mechanism_description.currency_of_support: PLN — contracts denominated in PLN. EUR-denominated investors face FX risk on support revenue unless hedged.
mechanism_description.fx_risk_note: PLN-denominated CfD creates FX exposure for EUR investors. PLN is not in ERM II and has historically been volatile vs. EUR. Model with FX assumptions; verify hedging availability.

auction.administrator: URE (organises and runs auctions per Ministerial Regulation)
auction.frequency: Annual — typically held in November/December for the following year

clearing_prices_pln_mwh_approx._staleness_warning: OZE clearing prices change annually. These are indicative 2024 ranges in PLN. Convert to EUR at prevailing rate. Verify with URE annual auction results.

phase_1_offshore_contracts.mechanism: Administratively set contract prices under Ustawa offshore 2020 — NOT competitive auction. Price set by Minister Klimatu in rozporządzenie.
phase_1_offshore_contracts.note: Phase 1 offshore contracts provide guaranteed price for 25 years (exceptional term vs. 15-year standard OZE). Prices significantly higher than onshore, reflecting project complexity.

tax._rule_3b_note: Rule 3B: Poland's CIT Act allows accelerated depreciation for certain assets. Wind turbine property tax reform (2023) significantly reduced the non-income tax burden on wind developers — model post-reform rates only for new projects.

depreciation.method: Straight-line (metoda liniowa) or declining balance (metoda degresywna) available
depreciation.legal_basis: Ustawa CIT Art. 16h–16m; Załącznik 1 (depreciation rates table)

npv_benefit_analysis.description: At 19% CIT and 10% WACC: NPV of tax shield under declining balance (2× coefficient on 7% linear rate = 14% year 1 DDB for wind): ≈ 14.3% of qualifying CapEx. Under standard linear 7% rate: NPV ≈ 11.8%. Timing benefit ≈ 2.5ppts. This is a modest incentive — CIT rate is lower than Western European peers which reduces the absolute shield value.

property_tax_on_wind_turbines.pre_2017_basis: Only foundation (fundament) treated as budowla (structure) for property tax purposes
property_tax_on_wind_turbines.2017_amendment: Act of 7 June 2018 (implementing earlier legislative interpretation from 2016) treated the ENTIRE wind turbine (including rotor, nacelle, tower) as 'budowla' — massively increasing property tax base

2023_reversal.description: Ustawa z dnia 13 lipca 2023 r. o zmianie ustawy o inwestycjach w zakresie elektrowni wiatrowych — restored wind turbine property tax base to FOUNDATION ONLY (fundament), dramatically reducing the annual tax burden
2023_reversal.legal_basis: Ustawa z dnia 13 lipca 2023 r. (Dz.U. 2023 poz. 1506)
2023_reversal.rate: Standard budowla rate: 2% of initial value per annum (set by gmina, up to statutory maximum)
2023_reversal.impact: For a 3MW wind turbine: pre-reform property tax was approx. PLN 90,000+/year (full turbine at 2% of ~PLN 4.5m initial value). Post-reform (foundation only at ~PLN 200,000 value): PLN 4,000/year. Orders of magnitude improvement.

property_tax_on_wind_turbines.current_basis: FOUNDATION ONLY for new projects. Verify legislative status — confirm 2023 amendment is in force and no further changes.

vat.legal_basis: Ustawa z dnia 11 marca 2004 r. o podatku od towarów i usług (VAT Act)

pcc.description: Podatek od czynności cywilnoprawnych (PCC) — Civil Law Activities Tax: 2% on market value of real property transferred; 1% on company shares
pcc.note: Relevant for site acquisitions and M&A structures.

ppa_market.legal_basis: Kodeks cywilny (Polish Civil Code) — Art. 353 et seq. (freedom of contract). No bespoke PPA statute.
ppa_market.prominent_corporate_offtakers: Multi-nationals with Polish operations (Amazon AWS PL, Google, ArcelorMittal Poland, PKN Orlen own renewables, industrial manufacturers)
ppa_market.oze_kontrakt_interaction_note: OZE two-way CfD contracts require surplus market revenue (above cena aukcyjna) to be returned to URE. A PPA layered on top must be structured as a virtual/financial arrangement — physical delivery under OZE contract is to TGE market, not directly to corporate offtaker.
ppa_market.pln_currency_risk: PLN-denominated OZE support and TGE spot market create FX risk for EUR-denominated investors AND for EUR-denominated corporate offtakers. PPA contracts may need FX provisions.

go_system.description: Gwarancje Pochodzenia (GP) — Polish Guarantees of Origin
go_system.regulator: URE (as GP issuing authority per Ustawa OZE)
go_system.notes: GP certificates available for renewable generators. Revenue from GP sales is modest (market-determined).

ppa_market.cross_border_enforceability: Medium — Polish courts are functional; commercial arbitration at SAKIG (Sąd Arbitrażowy przy Krajowej Izbie Gospodarczej) or ICC; post-EU accession legal framework has strengthened

credit_ratings.source_note: Poland's ratings improved significantly post-EU accession. Ratings were under pressure during 2015-2023 PiS government period due to rule of law concerns. Tusk government (from December 2023) has taken steps to restore EC rule of law approvals.

sovereign_risk.government_change: Coalition government under PM Donald Tusk (October 2023 Sejm election; sworn in December 2023) replaced PiS government of 8 years. Policy direction has shifted significantly.

rule_of_law_concerns.background: EC initiated infringement proceedings and froze RRF (Recovery and Resilience Fund) funds over judiciary independence concerns under PiS (2015-2023)
rule_of_law_concerns.current_status: Tusk government implementing judicial reform milestones. EC released initial frozen RRF tranches in 2024. Full rule of law restoration is ongoing — verify current EC/CJEU status.
rule_of_law_concerns.investor_relevance: Judicial independence concerns affect contract enforceability risk assessment and political risk premium for all sectors.

retroactive_policy_incidents.description: Ustawa wiatrakowa (May 2016) — introduced 10H setback rule retroactively constraining projects in the development pipeline. Projects with environmental permits but not yet under construction lost their development value.
retroactive_policy_incidents.severity: High for the onshore wind sector — effectively halted new onshore wind development for 7 years

retroactive_policy_incidents.description: Property tax reinterpretation treating entire wind turbine (not just foundation) as 'budowla' — effectively retroactively increased property tax burden for existing operational wind farms
retroactive_policy_incidents.severity: High for existing wind fleet — reversed by 2023 amendment but operating companies experienced 5+ years of elevated property tax

sovereign_risk.currency_risk: Medium — PLN is not in ERM II and has historical volatility vs. EUR. Polish exports and EU fund inflows provide some stabilisation. EUR/PLN rate has been in 4.0–4.80 range over recent years.
sovereign_risk.score_rationale: Poland's score (74) reflects improving trajectory under Tusk government but historical 10H and property tax retroactive measures are genuine negative precedents. PLN currency risk is a material consideration for EUR-base investors.

grid_connection_drag.assessment: HIGH — Poland faces a severe connection queue at distribution level; coal grid infrastructure requires transformation; PSE expansion plans are multi-year

permitting_drag_solar.assessment: MEDIUM — MPZP requirement is the primary constraint; OOŚ process is manageable; administration improving

permitting_drag_wind.assessment: MEDIUM — 700m reform opens the market but depends on gmina-by-gmina MPZP adoption. Expected 2024-2027 to reach full effect. Species protection (ptaki — birds) assessments required.

permitting_drag_offshore.assessment: MEDIUM — Phase 1 projects have pre-allocated contracts; Phase 2 competitive. Port/supply chain infrastructure the binding constraint more than permitting.

bureaucratic_friction.assessment: MEDIUM-HIGH — Polish administrative procedures involve multiple levels (gmina, starostwo, RDOŚ, URE). Processes are improving under Tusk government reforms but legacy complexity remains.

judicial_appeal_risk.assessment: MEDIUM — Polish administrative courts (WSA/NSA) function; no particular pattern of organised renewable energy opposition litigation unlike France/Italy/Germany

operational_drag.primary_bottleneck: Grid connection queue at distribution level (for solar) and MPZP adoption timelines at gmina level (for onshore wind 700m reform implementation)
operational_drag.score_rationale: Composite 65 reflects improving permitting landscape post-10H reform, but grid connection constraints are severe and will take years to resolve. PLN currency adds complexity vs. EUR-market peers.

fdi_restrictions.screening_regime: Ustawa o kontroli niektórych inwestycji (Investment Control Act 2015, as amended 2020/2022); EU FDI Screening Regulation (EU) 2019/452
fdi_restrictions.scope: Energy generation and distribution is included in the list of strategic sectors under the Act
fdi_restrictions.reviewing_authority: Prezes UOKiK (Office of Competition and Consumer Protection) for antitrust; sectoral ministry for strategic investment screening
fdi_restrictions.notes: Poland is an EU member with FDI screening for strategic sectors. Energy is covered. OECD investors face low prohibition risk but compliance required. Russia-affiliated investors effectively excluded from energy sector acquisitions post-2022.

eu_state_aid.oze_clearance: OZE auction mechanism cleared by EC under CEEAG. Competitive bidding (aukcje OZE) satisfies proportionality test.
eu_state_aid.phase_1_offshore_note: Phase 1 offshore administratively set prices reviewed by EC — clearance required as non-competitive mechanism. EC reviewed under CEEAG and issued compatibility decision.
eu_state_aid.retrospective_clawback_risk: Low for OZE auction projects — competitive mechanism is cleared. Phase 1 offshore administrative prices carry marginally higher clawback risk given non-competitive setting.
eu_state_aid.notification_status: OZE auction framework cleared; Phase 1 offshore cleared under specific EC decision

financial_model_inputs._rule_3d_note: Rule 3D: All Polish financial inputs should be modelled in PLN first, then converted to EUR at assumed FX rate with sensitivity analysis. PLN/EUR FX risk is material and must be reflected in IRR/NPV sensitivity tables.
financial_model_inputs._rule_3a_note: TWO-WAY CfD: model revenue as floored AND capped at cena aukcyjna. PLN-denominated floor — model EUR equivalent with FX sensitivity.

permitting_solar.rationale: MPZP requirement adds time but process is manageable. Grid connection queue is the binding constraint for solar.

permitting_wind_onshore.rationale: 700m reform (March 2023) reopens the market — but MPZP adoption is gmina-by-gmina and will take 2024-2027 to reach full effect across the country. Improving trajectory.

permitting_offshore.rationale: Phase 1 contracts pre-allocated. Phase 2 competitive from 2025. Port/supply chain infrastructure more limiting than regulatory process.

grid_connection.rationale: Severe distribution grid connection queue. Solar growth has saturated distribution capacity. Multi-year resolution timeline.

support_mechanism_availability.rationale: Annual OZE auctions active; 15-year two-way CfD contracts provide long-term revenue certainty; URE process is established and well-functioning. Phase 1 offshore contracts are exceptional 25-year term.

tax_efficiency.rationale: CIT 19% is competitive. Property tax post-2023 reform restored to foundation-only basis — dramatic improvement for wind. PLN denomination of tax regime is manageable. No Full Expensing equivalent.

ppa_enforceability.rationale: Polish contract law is sound; PPA market is growing; PLN/EUR FX adds complexity for corporate PPAs. Rule of law improvement under Tusk government reduces enforcement risk.

political_risk.rationale: A2/A- rated; EU member; 10H and property tax retroactive precedents; rule of law concerns improving post-December 2023. Trajectory is positive but 7-year PiS legacy is a due diligence item.

operational_drag.rationale: Grid is RED-level; permitting improving; MPZP timelines moderate. Offshore drag is moderate. Composite AMBER with improving trend.

fdi_restrictions.rationale: Investment Control Act applies to energy; notification required; OECD investors face low prohibition risk. Poland is strategically welcoming Western investment in energy post-2022 security context.

eu_state_aid_risk.rationale: OZE auction mechanism cleared under CEEAG. Competitive bidding is compliant.

rag_matrix.overall_rationale: Poland represents one of the most compelling emerging energy CapEx opportunities in this knowledge base. The onshore wind market reopening (700m reform), exceptional solar resource utilisation trajectory, and ambitious offshore programme create significant deal flow. Key risks are grid connection saturation, PLN currency exposure, and the legacy of retroactive policy under PiS (10H, property tax) — which have now been reversed but create a due diligence requirement. Investors comfortable with PLN FX exposure, emerging-market-style regulatory complexity, and 3–5 year development timelines will find Poland highly attractive relative to more saturated Western European markets.

comparison_table.1_permitting_window_scope: Onshore wind (post-700m reform, dependent on MPZP adoption)
comparison_table.3_primary_support_mechanism: OZE Aukcje — competitive auctions (URE); two-way CfD (kontrakt różnicowy)
comparison_table.4_key_incentive: Property tax reform (July 2023) restoring wind turbine tax to foundation-only basis — significant OPEX reduction. Standard CIT 19% with declining balance depreciation available.
comparison_table.5_ppa_legal_basis: Kodeks cywilny; TGE as reference price; PLN denomination creates FX risk for EUR-base investors
comparison_table.8_revenue_floor_native: PLN ~360/MWh approx. (onshore wind, indicative 2024 range — verify with URE)
comparison_table.8_revenue_floor_eur: EUR ~84.6/MWh approx. at PLN 4.25 = EUR 1 — FX-SENSITIVE
comparison_table.8_revenue_floor_staleness_warning: OZE clearing prices and PLN/EUR rate both change. Verify URE auction results AND current FX rate before use. PLN/EUR sensitivity analysis is mandatory.

disclaimer: This output is produced by Hyperion AI for indicative jurisdiction-screening purposes only. It does not constitute legal or financial advice. Users should obtain qualified local legal and financial counsel before committing capital. Hyperion AI complies with SRA Standards and Regulations (15/12/24 edition).

green_hydrogen.primary_support_mechanism: Poland participates in IPCEI Hy2Tech (2022). Polish Hydrogen Strategy 2030 targets 2 GW electrolysis capacity. NFOŚiGW (National Fund for Environmental Protection) administers EU-funded H2 support.
green_hydrogen.notes: Poland is an IPCEI Hy2Tech participant. Hydrogen strategy prioritises industrial decarbonisation (steel, chemicals) and transport. Grid decarbonisation path affects electrolysis carbon intensity credentials. Verify current subsidy programme before use.

floating_solar.primary_support_mechanism: OZE (Odnawialne Źródła Energii) two-way CfD auction under Ustawa o odnawialnych źródłach energii (Dz.U. 2015 poz. 478 as amended), administered by URE.
floating_solar.notes: Permitting estimated as ground-mounted solar P50 plus 9 months for water authority consent. Irradiance comparable to ground-mounted at same latitude per JRC PVGIS. Verify national tender eligibility before use.

nuclear_smr.notes: Poland has two parallel nuclear programmes: (1) large AP1000 units (Westinghouse — government programme, Choczewo site); (2) KGHM NuScale VOYGR SMR project (Legnica/Nowa Sól sites). Both at pre-construction stage. No operating nuclear in Poland. Source: IEA nuclear.

lng_gas.notes: Świnoujście LNG terminal (Gaz-System). Expansion underway. Poland has prioritised LNG as strategic energy security asset.

data_centre.notes: Warsaw is the main CEE data centre hub. Grid investment ongoing. Corporate PPA market developing. Source: national_tso.

natural_gas_lng.primary_support_mechanism: Polish Capacity Market — Rynek mocy (since 2021) under Ustawa o rynku mocy (Dz.U. 2018 poz. 9). CCGT and gas peaker plants are eligible. Source: URE/PSE.
natural_gas_lng.notes: Poland operates a formal Capacity Market (Rynek mocy) since 2021. Gas-fired plants are eligible capacity market participants and key to coal-replacement strategy. EU Taxonomy Delegated Act (EU 2022/1214) transition eligibility applies for qualifying CCGT below 270gCO2e/kWh. CCGT CapEx estimate: EUR 900,000/MW (IEA mid-point reference).

biowaste_energy.notes: Insufficient published data for medium-confidence assessment. EU RED III compliance and feedstock sustainability criteria apply. Verify current Polish bioenergy support under OZE auction framework (URE) before use.

coal_energy.primary_support_mechanism: Capacity Market (2021). Coal generators participate in multi-year contracts. Source: Polish Power Exchange (TGE).
coal_energy.coal_phase_out_commitment: 2049 target per Polish National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP); subject to EU pressure for earlier phase-out.
coal_energy.notes: Poland has Europe's most coal-intensive power sector (~70% coal share). EU Taxonomy-excluded. High stranded-asset risk post-2035. CBAM exposure for coal-intensive industries.

lh2_storage._overview: Liquid hydrogen (LH2) storage infrastructure: cryogenic storage at -253°C and associated liquefaction plant. Poland is an emerging hydrogen market anchored by PKN Orlen's 1 GW electrolysis ambition and EU-backed financing (KPO, FEnIKS). The primary safety regulatory gate is the SEVESO III regime administered via WIOŚ and PSP. Critically, Poland's electricity mix remains approximately 70% coal-based — grid-powered electrolysis will not meet green hydrogen carbon intensity thresholds without a dedicated renewable PPA. A co-located or dedicated PPA is essential for credible green LH2 credentials. Currency is PLN — EUR-base investors must model FX exposure on OpEx and grant receipts.
lh2_storage.primary_support_mechanism: No dedicated LH2 storage CfD or FiT. OZE auction mechanism does not cover LH2 storage. Standalone facilities operate on a tolling basis. CapEx grant support may be available via: (i) Fundusz Modernizacji (Modernisation Fund) — EU-backed fund targeting coal-region transition including H2 projects; (ii) FEnIKS (Fundusze Europejskie na Infrastrukturę, Klimat, Środowisko) 2021–2027 programme — EU structural funds for energy infrastructure; (iii) Krajowy Plan Odbudowy (KPO — National Recovery and Resilience Plan) — hydrogen component includes investment subsidies. Source: Ministerstwo Klimatu i Środowiska; NFOŚiGW; KPO programme documentation.
lh2_storage.staleness_warning: KPO grant call parameters, FEnIKS programme terms, and PLN/EUR exchange rate are all time-sensitive. Verify current call documentation with NFOŚiGW (Narodowy Fundusz Ochrony Środowiska i Gospodarki Wodnej) and Ministerstwo Funduszy i Polityki Regionalnej before use.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 27 kwietnia 2001 r. Prawo ochrony środowiska (Environmental Protection Law)
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: Primary framework transposing SEVESO III into Polish law. Operators of establishments handling dangerous substances at or above threshold quantities must notify WIOŚ and prepare a Raport o bezpieczeństwie (Safety Report) and Wewnętrzny Plan Operacyjno-Ratowniczy (internal emergency plan). For upper-tier: Zewnętrzny Plan Operacyjno-Ratowniczy (external emergency plan) coordinated by PSP.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Rozporządzenie Ministra Środowiska z dnia 30 grudnia 2002 r. w sprawie poważnych awarii przemysłowych (SEVESO III implementing regulation)
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: Sets specific threshold quantities for dangerous substances including flammable gases (wodór ciekły — liquid hydrogen). Lower tier: 10 tonnes LH2. Upper tier: 50 tonnes LH2. These are identical to EU SEVESO III baseline thresholds.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 24 sierpnia 1991 r. o ochronie przeciwpożarowej (Fire Protection Act)
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: PSP (Państwowa Straż Pożarna — State Fire Service) is the co-competent authority alongside WIOŚ for emergency planning at SEVESO establishments. PSP coordinates the external emergency plan (Zewnętrzny Plan Operacyjno-Ratowniczy) for upper-tier sites.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Dyrektywa 2012/18/UE (SEVESO III) transposed via Prawo ochrony środowiska
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: EU SEVESO III Directive basis; Poland as EU member is required to implement in full. SEVESO thresholds for flammable gases (hydrogen) are: lower tier 10 t; upper tier 50 t. Confirm no national variation has been adopted.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 7 lipca 1994 r. Prawo budowlane (Construction Law)
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: Pozwolenie na budowę (building permit) required for cryogenic storage structures; specific requirements for classified industrial facilities.

planning_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 27 marca 2003 r. o planowaniu i zagospodarowaniu przestrzennym
planning_legislation.relevance: Spatial planning law; LH2 facilities require compatibility with MPZP (Miejscowy Plan Zagospodarowania Przestrzennego — local spatial plan) or individual planning decision (decyzja WZ). Industrial/special economic zone sites are typically already designated for industrial use.

planning_legislation.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 3 października 2008 r. o udostępnianiu informacji o środowisku... (OOŚ Act)
planning_legislation.relevance: Environmental impact assessment (Ocena Oddziaływania na Środowisko — OOŚ) required for chemical/industrial facilities above qualifying thresholds; large LH2 installations will trigger OOŚ.

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: Polska Strategia Wodorowa do roku 2030 z perspektywą do 2040 (Polish Hydrogen Strategy 2021–2030)
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: National policy framework targeting 2 GW electrolysis capacity by 2030; identifies LH2 as a key transport and storage vector; designates hydrogen valleys (doliny wodorowe) as cluster development zones.

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: Krajowy Plan Odbudowy i Zwiększania Odporności (KPO — Poland's National Recovery and Resilience Plan)
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: EU RRF-funded programme. Component C4 (green energy and energy efficiency reduction) includes hydrogen investment subsidies. KPO grants may fund LH2-enabling infrastructure.

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: Program FEnIKS (Fundusze Europejskie na Infrastrukturę, Klimat, Środowisko 2021–2027)
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: EU structural and cohesion fund programme; energy priority axis covers hydrogen projects. NFOŚiGW is the managing institution for energy components.

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: Ustawa z dnia 10 maja 2018 r. o Centralnym Porcie Komunikacyjnym
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: CPK infrastructure investment programme has hydrogen logistics component — relevant context for LH2 transport infrastructure in Poland.

comah_equivalent_regime.regime_name: SEVESO III — implemented via Prawo ochrony środowiska
comah_equivalent_regime.competent_authority_primary: WIOŚ — Wojewódzki Inspektorat Ochrony Środowiska (Voivodship Inspectorate for Environmental Protection) — primary notification and inspection authority
comah_equivalent_regime.competent_authority_secondary: PSP — Państwowa Straż Pożarna (State Fire Service) — emergency planning co-authority for upper-tier establishments
comah_equivalent_regime.lower_tier_obligations: Notify WIOŚ; prepare and implement Polityka Zapobiegania Poważnym Awariom (PZPA — Major Accident Prevention Policy); prepare Wewnętrzny Plan Operacyjno-Ratowniczy (WPOR — internal emergency plan)
comah_equivalent_regime.upper_tier_obligations: Prepare and submit Raport o bezpieczeństwie (Safety Report) to WIOŚ; Zewnętrzny Plan Operacyjno-Ratowniczy (ZPOR — external emergency plan) coordinated by PSP; public information duty; periodic WIOŚ inspection
comah_equivalent_regime.legal_basis: Prawo ochrony środowiska Art. 248–264; Rozporządzenie MS z dnia 30 grudnia 2002 r. — SEVESO threshold quantities. Liquid hydrogen falls within the category of extremely flammable gases (gazy skrajnie łatwopalne).
comah_equivalent_regime.notes: Operations at upper-tier SEVESO establishments must not commence until WIOŚ has assessed and accepted the Safety Report. WIOŚ assessment capacity varies by voivodship. For industrial park / special economic zone locations, pre-application consultation with the relevant WIOŚ office is strongly recommended. PSP will review external emergency plan in parallel.

permitting.planning_authority: Starostwo powiatowe (county office) — building permit; RDOŚ (Regionalna Dyrekcja Ochrony Środowiska) for OOŚ/EIA decision; WIOŚ as SEVESO competent authority
parallel_consent_tracks: OOŚ decision from RDOŚ — environmental impact assessment: 6–12 months
Pozwolenie na budowę (building permit) from Starostwo: 3–6 months (follows OOŚ)
SEVESO WIOŚ notification / Safety Report submission: 3–10 months post-submission
PSP co-ordination for external emergency plan: concurrent with WIOŚ assessment
Grid/industrial connection for liquefaction plant: via local OSD/PSE — timelines as per grid_connection section
permitting.psi_zone_advantage: Location within Polska Strefa Inwestycji (PSI — Polish Investment Zone) may streamline permitting via single investor service point (PSSE — Polska Agencja Inwestycji i Handlu) coordination. PSSE can assist with administrative coordination across voivodship authorities.
permitting.appeal_mechanism: Samorządowe Kolegium Odwoławcze (SKO) → Wojewódzki Sąd Administracyjny (WSA) → Naczelny Sąd Administracyjny (NSA)
permitting.notes: P50 assumes a lower-tier SEVESO facility (10–50 tonnes LH2) in an industrial zone with existing MPZP designation. Upper-tier sites (>50 t) add significant time for Safety Report preparation. Special economic zone and industrial park sites (Katowice, Gdańsk, Łódź, Wrocław) typically have stronger planning designation and shorter MPZP-related delays. Rural greenfield sites will require MPZP amendment — add 12–24 months.

electricity_connection.relevance: Liquefaction is electricity-intensive (~10–12 kWh per kg H2 liquefied). CRITICAL NOTE: Poland's electricity grid is approximately 70% coal-fired (2025 data — verify current Urząd Regulacji Energetyki statistics). Grid electricity is not eligible for green hydrogen carbon intensity thresholds under EU Delegated Regulation 2023/1184. A dedicated renewable PPA or co-located renewable generation is mandatory for LH2 to qualify as renewable hydrogen under EU rules.

liquefaction_specific_energy_kwh_per_kg_.source: IEA, The Future of Hydrogen, 2019, p.52

electricity_connection.grid_operator: PSE S.A. (Polskie Sieci Elektroenergetyczne) for transmission; local OSD (Operator Systemu Dystrybucyjnego) for distribution connections
electricity_connection.grid_carbon_intensity_warning: CRITICAL: Polish grid electricity has a carbon intensity of approximately 750–900 gCO2e/kWh (URE/KOBIZE data — verify current). This EXCEEDS the 70% GHG savings threshold required for 'renewable hydrogen' under EU Delegated Regulation 2023/1184. LH2 from Polish grid electricity CANNOT be classified as green hydrogen without a dedicated renewable additionality structure.
electricity_connection.renewable_ppa_strategy: Co-located wind or solar PPA is MANDATORY for green LH2 credentials. Poland's OZE auction market provides access to wind/solar PPAs. Northern Poland (Baltic offshore) and central Poland (wind belt) offer best co-location options for renewable electricity. Industrial electricity price in Poland: approximately EUR 80–120/MWh for large consumers (URE data — verify current).
electricity_connection.connection_queue_note: Poland faces a severe grid connection queue at distribution level — see grid_connection section. LH2 liquefaction plant requiring a dedicated high-voltage connection should plan for PSE or OSD connection timelines of 24–60 months depending on voltage level and area congestion.
electricity_connection.notes: At EUR 0.10/kWh and 11 kWh/kg, electricity cost is EUR 1.10/kg LH2. A dedicated renewable PPA at EUR 0.06/kWh reduces this to EUR 0.66/kg. PLN-denominated industrial electricity is subject to PLN/EUR FX risk for EUR-reporting investors.

support_mechanisms._rule_3a_note: Rule 3A: No dedicated LH2 storage two-way CfD, one-way FiT, or auction mechanism exists in Poland. LH2 storage revenue is derived from tolling contracts (opłata za usługi skraplania i magazynowania — liquefaction and storage service fee). State support is CapEx grant only via EU-funded programmes.
support_mechanisms.primary_mechanism_standalone: Tolling arrangement — infrastructure fee for liquefaction, storage, and dispensing services; no commodity price floor from the state

kpo_component.description: KPO (Krajowy Plan Odbudowy) Component C4 — Green energy transition. Hydrogen infrastructure investment subsidies available for qualifying electrolysis and storage projects.
kpo_component.administrator: NFOŚiGW (Narodowy Fundusz Ochrony Środowiska i Gospodarki Wodnej)
kpo_component.indicative_grant_rate: Grant rate project-specific; KPO energy components have provided 25–50% of eligible CapEx in published call documentation (verify current call prospectus)
kpo_component.staleness_warning: KPO call parameters are revised per competition round. Poland's KPO disbursement has been subject to rule of law conditions — verify current EC disbursement approvals with NFOŚiGW before use.

feniks_component.description: FEnIKS (Fundusze Europejskie na Infrastrukturę, Klimat, Środowisko 2021–2027) — EU structural fund programme. Priority axis for low-emission energy includes hydrogen infrastructure.
feniks_component.administrator: NFOŚiGW / Ministerstwo Funduszy i Polityki Regionalnej
feniks_component.staleness_warning: FEnIKS call schedule and grant rates are published periodically. Verify current call with NFOŚiGW.

fundusz_modernizacji.description: Fundusz Modernizacji (Modernisation Fund) — EU Innovation Fund mechanism funding clean energy transition in coal-dependent member states including Poland. Relevant for LH2 in coal-region contexts (e.g., Bełchatów H2 valley concept).
fundusz_modernizacji.source: European Commission / NFOŚiGW Modernisation Fund documentation

psi_cit_exemption.description: Polska Strefa Inwestycji (PSI) — Polish Investment Zone. LH2 facilities in PSI-designated zones may qualify for CIT exemption (zwolnienie z podatku dochodowego) on qualifying investment. Exemption can cover up to 50% of eligible CapEx in certain assisted regions (maximum aid intensity per EU regional aid map — verify with PAIIH).
psi_cit_exemption.legal_basis: Ustawa z dnia 10 maja 2018 r. o wspieraniu nowych inwestycji (New Investment Support Act); Rozporządzenie Rady Ministrów z dnia 28 sierpnia 2018 r. w sprawie pomocy publicznej udzielanej niektórym przedsiębiorcom na realizację nowych inwestycji
psi_cit_exemption.staleness_warning: PSI aid intensity limits and regional assistance map are reviewed periodically by EC under State Aid regional guidelines. Verify current aid intensity for target location with PAIIH (Polska Agencja Inwestycji i Handlu) before use.
psi_cit_exemption.note: PSI CIT exemption is a significant incentive that can materially improve project NPV — effectively a CIT holiday on qualifying investment rather than a timing benefit.

eu_hydrogen_bank.description: Poland is an EU member state — projects may apply to the EU Hydrogen Bank auction programme (administered by the European Commission / EIB) for production subsidies. EU Hydrogen Bank targets green hydrogen producers via competitive grants.
eu_hydrogen_bank.source: European Commission, EU Hydrogen Bank — ec.europa.eu/hydrogen-bank

tax._rule_3b_note: Rule 3B: Polish depreciation for LH2 storage plant and equipment should be assessed under Ustawa CIT Art. 16h–16m. No Full Expensing equivalent exists. PSI CIT exemption is the primary CapEx-related tax incentive — it is NOT a timing benefit but a permanent exemption on qualifying investment.

depreciation_available.method: Declining balance (metoda degresywna) — coefficient up to 2× linear rate under Ustawa CIT Art. 16k
depreciation_available.legal_basis: Ustawa CIT Art. 16h–16m; Załącznik 1 (depreciation rates table)
depreciation_available.npv_timing_benefit: At 19% CIT and 10% WACC: NPV of tax shield under 2× declining balance (first year 20% on LH2 plant) ≈ 15.8% of qualifying CapEx. Under standard linear 10%/annum: NPV ≈ 12.2%. Timing benefit ≈ 3.6ppts. This is a modest incentive — lower absolute value than UK Full Expensing due to lower CIT rate.

vat.recovery: Input VAT recoverable by VAT-registered operators (podatnicy VAT)

tax.note: PSI CIT exemption (up to 50% of qualifying CapEx covered as tax exemption) is far more impactful than depreciation timing for Polish LH2 projects. Priority for tax analysis: (1) confirm PSI eligibility and aid intensity for target location; (2) model CIT exemption period; (3) apply standard declining balance depreciation for non-exempt period.

safety_standards.cryogenic_vessel_standard: EN 1251 (Cryogenic vessels — static vacuum-insulated vessels) — harmonised standard applicable in Poland as EU member state; implemented via Ustawa z dnia 13 kwietnia 2016 r. o systemach oceny zgodności i nadzoru rynku (conformity assessment framework)
safety_standards.electrical_hazardous_area: ATEX zone classification required per Rozporządzenie Ministra Energii z dnia 28 sierpnia 2019 r. w sprawie bezpieczeństwa i higieny pracy przy urządzeniach energetycznych (energy equipment safety regulation); ATEX Directive 2014/34/UE implemented via Polish law

management_options: Catalytic combustion with EA permit under Prawo ochrony środowiska
On-site power generation as boiler/turbine fuel
boil_off_gas_management.regulatory_basis: BOG must be addressed in WIOŚ Safety Report as a major accident scenario. Atmospheric venting may require pozwolenie na emisję (emission permit) from WIOŚ. Poland's air quality regulations (Prawo ochrony środowiska Art. 155 et seq.) govern industrial gas releases.
boil_off_gas_management.environmental_note: Vented hydrogen is not a direct GHG but is a safety hazard. Polish emission permitting (pozwolenie zintegrowane or pozwolenie sektorowe) governs BOG management choices.

financial_model_inputs._rule_3d_note: Rule 3D: these inputs are for IRR, NPV, CapEx payback, and DSCR modelling. All figures shown first in PLN (native currency) then EUR equivalent at PLN 1 = EUR 0.235 (approximately EUR 1 = PLN 4.25) as of 2026-06-15. PLN/EUR FX sensitivity analysis is MANDATORY.
financial_model_inputs._rule_3c_note: FX rate: PLN 1 = EUR 0.235 as of 2026-06-15. PLN is volatile vs EUR. Apply minimum sensitivity range of PLN 3.90–4.60 per EUR in scenario analysis.

illustrative_medium_scale_hub.scale: 2 tonne/day liquefaction capacity; 50 tonne vacuum-insulated storage

liquefaction_capex_pln.source: IEA, The Future of Hydrogen, 2019; Hydrogen Council, Hydrogen Insights 2023

storage_tank_capex_pln.notes: Vacuum-insulated Type C cryogenic tank; cost per tonne decreases with scale

illustrative_total_project_capex_pln.notes: Balance of plant and civil works estimated at 20% of equipment cost. Highly site-dependent. PSI CIT exemption may cover portion of qualifying CapEx — model separately.

opex_electricity.source: URE Polish industrial electricity price data — verify current. Renewable PPA rate may be lower (0.25–0.35 PLN/kWh at EUR 0.06–0.08/kWh) for dedicated green LH2 production.
opex_electricity.renewable_ppa_note: Green LH2 from dedicated renewable PPA: electricity cost approximately PLN 0.30/kWh = EUR 0.071/kWh. Electricity OpEx: EUR 0.78/kg LH2. Non-green (grid) at PLN 0.42/kWh: EUR 1.09/kg LH2.

illustrative_medium_scale_hub.note: All PLN figures converted at PLN 1 = EUR 0.235 (2026-06-15). Verify FX rate. PLN/EUR sensitivity of ±10% translates to ±10% on all PLN-denominated OpEx items. Engineering study required before financial modelling.

market_context.poland_policy_position: Polska Strategia Wodorowa 2030 targets 2 GW electrolysis by 2030. Poland is an IPCEI Hy2Tech participant. The dominant commercial actors are PKN Orlen (targeting 1 GW electrolyser capacity) and Lotos/PKN Orlen Gdańsk (refinery hydrogen hub). Bełchatów post-coal transition includes an H2 valley concept funded via Fundusz Modernizacji.

key_polish_lh2_projects_indicative.status: Development — Orlen committed to 1 GW electrolysis capacity across Poland and Czech Republic by 2030. Includes production, liquefaction, and distribution for transport.
key_polish_lh2_projects_indicative.note: Poland's dominant energy company. Orlen's hydrogen programme is the largest single pipeline in the Polish market.

key_polish_lh2_projects_indicative.status: Operational — Gdańsk refinery uses hydrogen for desulphurisation; integration of electrolytic H2 in development
key_polish_lh2_projects_indicative.note: Existing hydrogen demand at Gdańsk creates immediate market for locally produced LH2 storage and supply

key_polish_lh2_projects_indicative.status: Concept/early development — coal region transition concept linking decommissioned Bełchatów power plant site with hydrogen production and storage
key_polish_lh2_projects_indicative.note: Fundusz Modernizacji relevant; site has existing grid infrastructure

primary_demand_drivers: Heavy road transport (PKN Orlen developing LH2 refuelling network along Polish motorways — A1, A2, A4 corridors)
Industrial decarbonisation (steel — ArcelorMittal Poland; chemicals — Ciech, Anwil)
Rail transport (PKP intercity hydrogen train pilot concepts)
Export potential — proximity to German hydrogen demand via Baltic pipeline or ISO tanker
market_context.supply_chain_note: Poland has limited domestic LH2 production and no established commercial LH2 supply chain. Air Products and Linde operate industrial gas infrastructure. Electrolysis technology suppliers include Hydrogenious LOHC, Nel Hydrogen. CCGT gas infrastructure in Poland (Gaz-System) provides pipeline hydrogen transport context but not LH2 direct analogue.
market_context.grid_decarbonisation_note: Poland's ~70% coal electricity mix is the single biggest barrier to green LH2 credibility. Without a dedicated renewable PPA, LH2 produced from Polish grid electricity will not qualify as 'renewable fuel of non-biological origin' under EU Delegated Regulation 2023/1184 Article 3 (carbon intensity threshold). This is a fundamental constraint that must be addressed in project design before any financial commitment.

lh2_storage.notes: Poland is an emerging market for LH2 with strong policy ambition but structural constraints. The SEVESO III safety pathway via WIOŚ and PSP is clear but demanding. The most material investment risks are: (1) Grid decarbonisation — Polish coal-heavy electricity mix requires dedicated renewable PPA for green LH2 qualification; (2) PLN currency risk — all support grants, tariffs, and operational revenues are in PLN, creating material FX exposure for EUR-reporting investors; (3) PSI eligibility confirmation — the CIT exemption is a major incentive but location-dependent. KPO/FEnIKS grant availability has been subject to rule of law conditionality — verify current EC disbursement status with NFOŚiGW. All grant parameters, PLN/EUR rate, and industrial electricity prices must be verified before use.
